members.name: _Pressed
members.summary: Called when the button is pressed. If you need to know the button's pressed state (and [member toggle_mode] is active), use [method _toggled] instead.

members.name: _Toggled
members.summary: Called when the button is toggled (only if [member toggle_mode] is active).

members.name: GetDrawMode
members.summary: Returns the visual state used to draw the button. This is useful mainly when implementing your own draw code by either overriding _draw() or connecting to "draw" signal. The visual state of the button is defined by the [enum DrawMode] enum.

members.name: IsHovered
members.summary: Returns [code]true[/code] if the mouse has entered the button and has not left it yet.

members.name: SetPressedNoSignal
members.summary: Changes the [member button_pressed] state of the button, without emitting [signal toggled]. Use when you just want to change the state of the button without sending the pressed event (e.g. when initializing scene). Only works if [member toggle_mode] is [code]true[/code]. [b]Note:[/b] This method doesn't unpress other buttons in [member button_group].

members.name: ActionMode
members.summary: Determines when the button is considered clicked.

members.name: ButtonGroup
members.summary: The [ButtonGroup] associated with the button. Not to be confused with node groups. [b]Note:[/b] The button will be configured as a radio button if a [ButtonGroup] is assigned to it.

members.name: ButtonMask
members.summary: Binary mask to choose which mouse buttons this button will respond to. To allow both left-click and right-click, use [code]MOUSE_BUTTON_MASK_LEFT | MOUSE_BUTTON_MASK_RIGHT[/code].

members.name: ButtonPressed
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], the button's state is pressed. Means the button is pressed down or toggled (if [member toggle_mode] is active). Only works if [member toggle_mode] is [code]true[/code]. [b]Note:[/b] Changing the value of [member button_pressed] will result in [signal toggled] to be emitted. If you want to change the pressed state without emitting that signal, use [method set_pressed_no_signal].

members.name: Disabled
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], the button is in disabled state and can't be clicked or toggled. [b]Note:[/b] If the button is disabled while held down, [signal button_up] will be emitted.

members.name: KeepPressedOutside
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], the button stays pressed when moving the cursor outside the button while pressing it. [b]Note:[/b] This property only affects the button's visual appearance. Signals will be emitted at the same moment regardless of this property's value.

members.name: ShortcutFeedback
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], the button will highlight for a short amount of time when its shortcut is activated. If [code]false[/code] and [member toggle_mode] is [code]false[/code], the shortcut will activate without any visual feedback.

members.name: ShortcutInTooltip
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], the button will add information about its shortcut in the tooltip. This includes the shortcut's events and its [member Resource.resource_name]. If both events and name are empty, the shortcut will not be included. [b]Note:[/b] This property does nothing when the tooltip control is customized using [method Control._make_custom_tooltip].

members.name: ToggleMode
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], the button is in toggle mode. Makes the button flip state between pressed and unpressed each time its area is clicked.

signals.name: ButtonDown
signals.summary: Emitted when the button starts being held down.

signals.name: ButtonUp
signals.summary: Emitted when the button stops being held down.

signals.name: Pressed
signals.summary: Emitted when the button is toggled or pressed. This is on [signal button_down] if [member action_mode] is [constant ACTION_MODE_BUTTON_PRESS] and on [signal button_up] otherwise. If you need to know the button's pressed state (and [member toggle_mode] is active), use [signal toggled] instead.

signals.name: Toggled
signals.summary: Emitted when the button was just toggled between pressed and normal states (only if [member toggle_mode] is active). The new state is contained in the [param toggled_on] argument.

values.name: ACTION_MODE_BUTTON_PRESS
values.summary: Require just a press to consider the button clicked.

values.name: ACTION_MODE_BUTTON_RELEASE
values.summary: Require a press and a subsequent release before considering the button clicked.

values.name: DRAW_DISABLED
values.summary: The state of buttons are disabled.

values.name: DRAW_HOVER
values.summary: The state of buttons are hovered.

values.name: DRAW_HOVER_PRESSED
values.summary: The state of buttons are both hovered and pressed.

values.name: DRAW_NORMAL
values.summary: The normal state (i.e. not pressed, not hovered, not toggled and enabled) of buttons.

values.name: DRAW_PRESSED
values.summary: The state of buttons are pressed.

constants.name: DRAW_NORMAL
constants.summary: The normal state (i.e. not pressed, not hovered, not toggled and enabled) of buttons.

constants.name: DRAW_PRESSED
constants.summary: The state of buttons are pressed.

constants.name: DRAW_HOVER
constants.summary: The state of buttons are hovered.

constants.name: DRAW_DISABLED
constants.summary: The state of buttons are disabled.

constants.name: DRAW_HOVER_PRESSED
constants.summary: The state of buttons are both hovered and pressed.

constants.name: ACTION_MODE_BUTTON_PRESS
constants.summary: Require just a press to consider the button clicked.

constants.name: ACTION_MODE_BUTTON_RELEASE
constants.summary: Require a press and a subsequent release before considering the button clicked.

virtualMethods.name: _Pressed
virtualMethods.summary: Called when the button is pressed. If you need to know the button's pressed state (and [member toggle_mode] is active), use [method _toggled] instead.

virtualMethods.name: _Toggled
virtualMethods.summary: Called when the button is toggled (only if [member toggle_mode] is active).
